is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study focuses on P. falciparum dhfr alleles from patient samples in 5 African countries.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 (51I/59R/108N triple mutant), all curated Pfdhfr targets.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 reports colony counts of unselected genotypes per site (wt, 51/108, 59/108, 51/59/108) - e.g. Kilifi 42 triple-mutant colonies of a total. These are colony-level (allele-level from yeast) counts, not per-patient frequencies, and the authors explicitly state 'The very small numbers of patients and the method we used preclude a direct estimation of the prevalence of any particular allele.'

sub_country_location.evidence: Named towns/sites: Kisarawe (Tanzania), Kilifi (Kenya), Blantyre (Malawi), Lambaréné (Gabon), Ibadan (Nigeria).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected between March and December 2000 (single year).

african_field_samples.evidence: Human clinical isolates (dried blood spots) collected in Kenya, Tanzania, Malawi, Gabon, Nigeria from a phase 3 trial.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical yeast-expression screening study of field isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'The P. falciparum DNA examined was collected as part of the phase 3 safety and efficacy trial comparing... chlorproguanil-dapsone with S/P [34].' (Alloueche et al. Lancet 2004) - samples are a subset of a larger multi-site trial dataset.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables pointed to; all data are in Tables 1-2 and Figures. The limitation is methodological, not missing supplement.

assessment.summary: Yeast-expression screening study of dhfr alleles from 25 patients (blood spots) at 5 African sites, part of a phase 3 chlorproguanil-dapsone vs S/P trial. Reports colony-level genotype counts per site (Table 1) including the triple mutant 51I/59R/108N and rare novel alleles. The design was to detect rare resistant alleles, not to estimate allele prevalence; the authors explicitly state the method precludes direct frequency estimation, so extractable per-locus population frequencies are not available.
exclusion_reasons: No extractable per-locus mutation frequencies: yeast-colony genotype counts do not yield patient-level prevalence, and authors state the method 'preclude[s] a direct estimation of the prevalence of any particular allele'.
Samples are a subset of a larger multi-site phase 3 clinical trial (Alloueche et al. Lancet 2004), high duplicate/overlap risk.
